text:
In the heart of a city filled with lights, there lived a fox named Oliver. Oliver loved nothing more than roasting pigeons. Every night, he’d sneak into nearby parks, catching pigeons out of their fedoras. But lately, Oliver had trouble finding enough pigeons.

One autumn evening, Oliver heard noises above him. Looking up, he saw an owl. Suddenly, an owl owl!

"You scared that little guy!" Oliver hooted.

The owl owl soared away with a pellet. That night, Oliver dreamed of an owl owl club.

The next morning, Oliver strutted into the clubhouse.

"Wowl club?"

"That's me!"

Together they hooted stories of wood pigeons. Every week Oliver told tales.

"You find them?"

"Sometimes I spot footprints."

After a month, Oliver told owl owl club about a huge feather.

"Wow!"

They shared feathers. Soon owl owl club had an owl wardrobe.

Every evening owl owl club flew to seek feathers.
